reasoning: The Lean statement exactly captures the informal claim. `Polynomial.Gal p` is defined in Mathlib as `p.SplittingField ≃ₐ[F] p.SplittingField`, so `Gal (X^4 - 2*X^2 - 2 : ℚ[X])` is the Galois group of the splitting field of x^4 - 2x^2 - 2 over ℚ. `DihedralGroup 4` has cardinality 2·4 = 8 (confirmed by `DihedralGroup.card`), which matches "the dihedral group with eight elements" (D₄). `Nonempty (_ ≃* _)` is the standard way in Mathlib to assert that two groups are isomorphic. The polynomial, ambient field, Galois group, and target dihedral group all match; no hypotheses are added or dropped.
